title: Open Buro Service Providers Manifest
description: A Service Provider manifest is the array of providers a consumer fetches in order to discover and resolve capabilities.

provider.description: A service that provides one or more capabilities.

id.description: Unique, stable, technical identifier for the provider, in reverse-DNS notation (lowercase, dot-separated, at least two labels).

name.description: Human-readable display name, e.g. shown in a chooser when several providers match.
name.$comment: Could be used by screen readers. TODO: Add support for localization.

url.description: Provider base URL. Informational entry point.

version.description: Version of the provider entry's format, used by the consumer to know how to parse the entry. A single manifest may mix providers of different versions.

icon.description: Optional URL to the provider's icon, shown in the chooser when several providers match.

target.description: Optional hint for how the consumer should open this provider's capability UI.
target.$comment: Could be a list with the default order of preference, so that a Service Provider could just list both

capabilities.description: The actions this provider can perform.

capability.description: A single action a provider can perform.

action.description: PICK: the consumer asks the provider for one file, many files, or a folder. SAVE: the consumer sends one file, many files, or a folder to the provider.

path.description: Endpoint that fulfils this capability. Either an absolute URL or a relative reference resolved against the provider `url`. Should return a UI for the user to pick or save files, and then return the picked or saved file(s) to the consumer.

mimeTypes.description: MIME filters the capability accepts (e.g. any file, or only images for a gallery). When omitted, the consumer falls back to ['*/*'].

multiple.description: Whether the capability can pick or save multiple files.

mimePattern.description: A full MIME type, a subtype wildcard (image/*), or the catch-all */*.
